steps.title: Apply the width increase
steps.content:
If the width increases by $6$, the new width is $W+6$. The new length must be
$$2.25(W+6)=2.25W+13.5$$

steps.title: Compare old and new lengths
steps.content: The original length was $2.25W$, and the new length is $2.25W+13.5$. So the length must increase by $13.5$ units. The answer is C.
